round_note: Refire on 2026-04-27 against the live tree. This witness supersedes the earlier same-path artifact and replaces stale broad grep counts with live workspace measurements.

alpha.consumed_as: background prior only; not load-bearing on Tier-1 promotion

beta.consumed_as: tie-break seed; no tie needed in this round

gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_lhc invocation=tool/lhc_opendata_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)
gamma.consumed_as: external bridge not invoked; offline fallback honored

core_5op_kernel: 3) fan out K>=3 orthogonal axes (raw 48 correlation < 0.7)
5) emit witness JSON to declared landing path

intake.question: Does porting or splitting extensions/types.ts produce enough ROI now to justify churn?
intake.interpretation: The file remains the extension contract nexus for UI, events, commands, runtime actions, tools, provider registration, and loader-facing runtime types. Any port decision is package-contract work, not an internal cleanup only.

dedupe_method: structural dedupe by failure-domain: package-contract pressure, runtime-loader coupling, consumer blast radius, change cadence, staged seam viability, and review-surface reduction collapsed to one representative pattern each

anchors: types.ts:79-82 re-export exec/system-prompt/agent-tool/keybinding types at the top of the file
types.ts:89-1548 defines the extension UI, context, event, tool, API, provider, runtime, and error contract surface
live scan counted 82 exported interfaces, 33 exported type aliases, and 17 exported functions
index.ts:51-130 re-exports the extension type surface from the package root
index.ts:131-146 re-exports extension runtime helpers from the same subtree
loader.ts:24-27 imports ../../index.js specifically so extensions can import from @need-singularity/hive-coding-agent without a circular dependency
loader.ts:32-42 imports Extension, ExtensionAPI, ExtensionFactory, ExtensionRuntime, LoadExtensionsResult, MessageRenderer, ProviderConfig, RegisteredCommand, and ToolDefinition from ./types.js
loader.ts:44-56 and 87-103 bind the package root and bundled modules into virtualModules and aliases

consumer_snapshots: /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/telegram/src/extension.ts imports AgentEndEvent, BeforeAgentStartEvent, ExtensionAPI, ExtensionCommandContext, and ExtensionContext from @need-singularity/hive-coding-agent

hypotheses.claim: A full port of extensions/types.ts now has positive ROI.
Only a staged seam-extraction or alias-preserving split has positive ROI.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: size-or-isolation-floor
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: If the file is small or isolated, a full port could be cheap enough to justify immediate action.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: public-contract-overstatement
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: If the package root does not publicly re-export this surface, churn risk is overstated.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: loader-agnosticity
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: If the loader does not directly depend on ./types.js or the package-root alias, the port blast radius is lower than claimed.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: consumer-floor
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: If only a few internal files consume the types directly, the downstream blast radius is modest.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: alias-preserving-split-impossible
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: If a staged split cannot preserve current import paths, the incremental strategy is not viable.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: cosmetic-only-seam
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: If the split does not reduce dependency fan-in or review burden, ROI is cosmetic.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: no-tier1-next-step
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: If there is no bounded next step short of a full port, the cycle should fail.

axes_fanout.question: How much user-visible API leverage is tied to this file?
axes_fanout.finding: High leverage. The package root re-exports 78 extension names from the extension subtree, so movement here is package-contract work and reaches outside the local module.
axes_fanout.roi_signal: Opposes abrupt full port; supports alias-preserving internals-only extraction.

axes_fanout.question: Will a move stay in the type layer, or does it touch runtime loading?
axes_fanout.finding: It touches runtime loading. loader.ts imports multiple runtime-relevant types from ./types.js and separately binds the package root into virtualModules and alias maps for extension resolution.
axes_fanout.roi_signal: Strong negative for a big-bang reorganization.

axes_fanout.question: How broadly is the surface consumed today?
axes_fanout.finding: Broad enough that compatibility capital matters. The live scan found 230 matching reference lines across 138 hive files, with 111 package-level importer files, plus direct usage in a sister package and tests.
axes_fanout.roi_signal: Further negative for wholesale movement; positive for a stable-barrel split.

candidate_options.name: Full port or wide split now
candidate_options.benefit: Conceptual cleanup only if the whole extension contract can be safely decomposed at once.

candidate_options.name: Extract one low-coupling subsection into sibling files while keeping types.ts as the barrel
candidate_options.benefit: Reduces local review burden and proves seam quality without spending package compatibility capital.

candidate_options.name: Leave the file intact
candidate_options.benefit: Avoids churn entirely, but leaves a 1553-LOC convergence file in place and learns nothing about actual seam quality.
candidate_options.verdict: acceptable-hold but lower learning ROI than OPT_B

falsifier_results.reason: The file is not small or isolated: 1553 LOC, 82 exported interfaces, 33 exported type aliases, 17 exported functions, and 21 unique import modules.
The package root publicly re-exports 78 extension-surface names through index.ts:51-146.
loader.ts directly imports runtime-relevant types from ./types.js and binds @need-singularity/hive-coding-agent into virtualModules and alias resolution.
Direct consumers span runtime code, tests, docs, examples, and a sister package; the live workspace scan found 138 matching hive files and 111 package-level importer files.
A staged split is viable because src/core/extensions/types.ts can remain the barrel while extracted leaf sections are re-exported under the same paths.

The file already contains separable contract clusters such as UI dialog/widget types, message rendering types, and event helper groups; extracting one of those lowers review surface without immediate contract churn.
There is a bounded Tier-1 next step: preserve existing import contracts, extract one low-risk section, and verify loader plus package-root exports with targeted tests.

tier_1_promotions.rationale: A full port is negative ROI today because the file is simultaneously a public contract, a runtime-loader dependency, and a wide internal dependency hub.
An additive split that preserves src/core/extensions/types.ts and package-root exports has positive ROI because it improves locality without spending compatibility capital.
Any movement must be gated by verification of loader imports, virtualModules exposure assumptions, and package-root re-exports; otherwise the work is churn disguised as cleanup.

decision.summary: Do not do a wholesale extensions/types.ts port now. The only positive-ROI path is a staged, alias-preserving split that keeps current consumer imports stable while proving one low-coupling seam.

allowed: Extract one low-coupling subsection into a sibling file
Keep src/core/extensions/types.ts as the stable barrel
Run targeted tests around loader imports and at least one package-root extension consumer
forbidden_until_reproven: Moving loader-critical names without alias compatibility
Big-bang reorganizations across examples, docs, and tests before one seam is validated

convergence_witness.evidence: The live tree falsifies the cheap-full-port hypothesis on three orthogonal axes: public contract pressure, runtime coupling, and downstream blast radius. Those same observations leave one bounded positive path: alias-preserving staged extraction with verification gates.
convergence_witness.omega_stop: Stop after the ROI question resolves to a stable action rule. Full port is negative ROI now; bounded alias-preserving extraction is the only Tier-1 admissible move.
